audience_insights:
- Buyers want a fast way to compare tiers without reading feature grids line by line
- Pricing anxiety spikes when the premium tier looks expensive before its context is clear
competitor_observations:
- Many SaaS pages list the middle tier first, which weakens anchor contrast
- Strong performers explain the upgrade trigger before they explain every feature
key_facts:
- Anchoring changes perceived value when the higher-price option is framed first
- Buyers compare relative differences more than absolute feature counts
proof_points:
- The decoy effect is strongest when one tier makes the target tier feel more reasonable
- Order and framing influence willingness to pay before detailed evaluation
examples:
- A pricing page that surfaces the premium tier outcome before the checklist
- A teardown showing how reordered plan cards changed demo quality
case_studies:
- A SaaS founder reduced discount requests after clarifying plan contrast
gaps_to_exploit:
- Few creators explain pricing psychology with concrete page examples
assets_needed:
- Screenshot of a three-tier pricing page
- Simple annotated mock showing anchor placement
claims_requiring_verification:
- Any quantified lift claim tied to pricing page changes
unsafe_or_uncertain_claims:
- Do not imply anchoring guarantees revenue growth
research_stop_reason: Enough evidence to build a practical teardown without hard performance claims
